title: Runtime Configuration, Secrets, Environment Precedence, and Sensitive Data · Runtime and Build Recall
summary: An operations reviewer reconstructs runtime configuration, secrets, environment precedence, and sensitive data from delayed evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and extends the configuration and secret-flow contract under a changed constraint. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for configuration and secret-flow contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate build arguments, build secrets, image environment, runtime environment, Compose interpolation, configs, and application configuration.
Resolve CLI, Compose, env_file, image, shell, quoting, unset, empty, required, default, and literal-dollar environment behavior.
Grant only named services access to declared secrets and configs and verify file path, permissions, rotation, and local Compose limitations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Docker Credential Helper Rotation
steps.instruction:
review case — A review build receives credentials through ARG and ENV; Compose interpolation prints them; image history and logs retain copies. Classify public config, build secrets, runtime secrets, redaction, and rotation. Competency probe: Use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive CI login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage.

Inspect the Docker Credential Helper Rotation trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Docker Credential Helper Rotation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive ci login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Docker Credential Helper Rotation, keep Docker Credential Helper Rotation connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Docker Credential Helper Rotation, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Docker Credential Helper Rotation, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Credential Helper Rotation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Docker Credential Helper Rotation, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A review build receives credentials through ARG and ENV; Compose interpolation prints them; image history and logs retain copies. Classify public config, build secrets, runtime secrets, redaction, and rotation. Competency probe: Use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive CI login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Docker Credential Helper Rotation, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: docker login credentials are always stored encrypted and cannot leak through process or CI output.

stimulus.title: Docker Credential Helper Rotation case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review build receives credentials through ARG and ENV; Compose interpolation prints them; image history and logs retain copies. Classify public config, build secrets, runtime secrets, redaction, and rotation. Competency probe: Use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive CI login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: docker login credentials are always stored encrypted and cannot leak through process or CI output.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive CI login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage. Artifact under review: configuration and secret-flow contract.

options.text: The Docker Credential Helper Rotation record says configuration and secret-flow contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Docker Credential Helper Rotation.

For separate evidence from confidence, copy one familiar Docker command, omit daemon authority, immutable identity, failure, cleanup, and disposable-environment transfer checks, then accept a successful build as production proof. This does not satisfy Docker Credential Helper Rotation for configuration and secret-flow contract.
The record names the assumption, applies Docker Credential Helper Rotation, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
